method: Direct fetch of primary source (mistral.ai/news/robostral-navigate) by verifier agent; independent web searches for third-party coverage, reproduction, and prior-art check on 'Robostral' branding; separate adversarial falsification pass by a second fresh agent (date/recycling check, R2R-CE benchmark legitimacy check, competitor-scoop check).
origin_items: Tier-0 sweep this cycle (Mistral AI News page, https://mistral.ai/news) — item dated 2026-07-08

claims.text: Mistral released Robostral Navigate on 8 July 2026, an 8B-parameter model that takes RGB images and a plain-language instruction and moves a robot through an environment using a single camera, no depth sensor or LiDAR required; runs on wheeled, legged, and flying robots
claims.reasoning: Primary source (Mistral's own blog post) fetched directly and quoted verbatim by the verifier. Independently corroborated same-day by Bloomberg, TestingCatalog, Verdict, and Technology.org with descriptions matching the primary source.

claims.text: On R2R-CE, Mistral reports 79.4% (seen) / 76.6% (unseen) success, beating the best single-camera approach by 9.7 points and the best depth/multi-camera system by 4.5 points
claims.reasoning: Figures traced to Mistral's own announcement only; no independent reproduction exists yet (expected for a same-week release). Adversarial pass confirmed R2R-CE is a real, established indoor-navigation benchmark (Room-to-Room Continuous Environments) and that recent published single-camera SOTA sits near 65%, so the claimed jump is large but plausible, not a fabricated record. Labeled vendor-claim per verification.md §3.

claims.text: Trained on ~400,000 simulated trajectories across 6,000 scenes; a prefix-caching technique cut training tokens by 22x, compressing training time from months to days
claims.reasoning: Infrastructure/methodology claim, self-reported, not externally testable pre-open-source-release. No independent source found or expected.

claims.text: This is Mistral's first robotics model
claims.reasoning: Primary source itself frames this as Mistral's own first embodied-navigation model (company-scoped 'first', not an industry-first claim). Independently corroborated by multiple outlets (Yahoo/Tech, Digital Today, GlobalBanking&Finance, TipRanks) explicitly calling it Mistral's first robotics model. Checked for a prior 'Robostral'-branded release — none found (a differently-named March 2026 teaser, 'WMa1', and a May 2026 Emmi AI acquisition are background, not a prior Robostral product).

notes: Adversarial falsification pass (fresh agent): confirmed the 8 July 2026 date is genuine (not recycled), cross-checked via Bloomberg and Mistral's own X post. No fraud/fabrication allegations found; legitimate technical caveats exist (monocular vision struggles with textureless/reflective surfaces, sim-to-real gap since training was 100% simulated, 76.6% unseen-env success rate implies roughly 1-in-4 failure rate) and are reflected in the article's 'why it matters' section rather than hidden. NVIDIA had a broader, concurrent robotics announcement week (Cosmos 3, Isaac GR00T N1.6) but in a different category (general world-foundation models vs. Mistral's narrower navigation model) — does not contradict or scoop Mistral's company-scoped 'first' claim; not included as a load-bearing comparison in the article. R2R-CE is confirmed as a real benchmark via arXiv cross-reference (CorrectNav 65.1%, Efficient-VLN 64.2%, RynnBrain-Nav-8B 58.6%, BudVLN 57.6% for recent single-camera systems).
